articles.title: When made to feel sad, men with psychopathic traits shift their visual focus to anger
articles.titleKo: 슬픔을 느끼게 되면 사이코패스 성향의 남성들은 시선을 분노로 옮긴다
articles.summary: Are psychopaths completely incapable of feeling sad? A new study of incarcerated men suggests otherwise, revealing that psychopathy may actually be a subconscious defense mechanism where the brain actively avoids sadness and hyper-focuses on anger instead.
articles.aiSummary:
This study challenges the conventional belief that psychopaths are completely incapable of experiencing sadness. A new study of incarcerated men suggests that psychopathy may not be a simple emotional deficit but rather an unconscious defense mechanism where the brain actively avoids sadness and hyper-focuses on anger instead. Researchers conducted an experiment inducing sadness in participants and measured changes in their visual focus. The key finding revealed that men with higher psychopathic traits shifted their visual attention more toward anger-related stimuli when experiencing sadness. This suggests psychopathy involves active emotion avoidance rather than emotional incapacity. These findings could reshape our understanding of the psychological mechanisms underlying psychopathy. However, the study's limitation lies in its sample being restricted to incarcerated individuals, which may limit generalizability to the general population.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 정신병질적 특성을 가진 사람들이 슬픔을 느낄 수 없다는 기존의 통념에 의문을 제기합니다. 교도소에 수감 중인 남성들을 대상으로 한 신규 연구는 정신병질이 단순히 감정 결핍이 아니라, 뇌가 무의식적으로 슬픔을 회피하고 분노에 과도하게 집중하는 방어 기제일 수 있음을 시사합니다. 연구원들은 참가자들에게 슬픈 감정을 유도한 후 시각적 초점 변화를 측정하는 실험을 진행했습니다. 핵심 결과는 정신병질적 특성이 높은 남성들이 슬픔을 느낄 때 분노 관련 자극으로 시각을 더 많이 옮긴다는 것입니다. 이는 정신병질이 감정 무능력이 아닌 적극적인 감정 회피 메커니즘임을 시사합니다. 이 발견은 정신병질자의 심리 기제에 대한 이해를 재구성할 수 있습니다. 다만 표본이 수감자로 제한되어 일반 인구에 대한 적용 가능성에는 한계가 있습니다.

articles.title: UQ-SHRED: uncertainty quantification of shallow recurrent decoder networks for sparse sensing via engression
articles.titleKo: UQ-SHRED: 엥그레션을 통한 희소 센싱을 위한 얕은 순환 디코더 네트워크의 불확실성 정량화
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.01305v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reconstructing high-dimensional spatiotemporal fields from sparse sensor measurements is critical in a wide range of scientific applications. The SHallow REcurrent Decoder (SHRED) architecture is a recent state-of-the-art architecture that reconstructs high-quality spatial domain from hyper-sparse sensor measurement streams. An important limitation of SHRED is that in complex, data-scarce, high-frequency, or stochastic systems, portions of the spatiotemporal field must be modeled with valid uncertainty estimation. We introduce UQ-SHRED, a distrib
articles.aiSummary:
This study addresses the challenge of reconstructing high-dimensional spatiotemporal fields from sparse sensor measurements, which is critical in many scientific applications. While the existing SHRED (SHallow REcurrent Decoder) architecture successfully reconstructs spatial information from minimal sensor data, it lacks the ability to quantify prediction uncertainty in complex or data-scarce environments. The researchers propose UQ-SHRED, which enhances the original architecture by incorporating uncertainty quantification through probabilistic distributions. This advancement enables machine learning models to provide numerical estimates of prediction reliability alongside their forecasts. The method has significant implications for applications such as climate modeling, turbulent flow analysis, and other scientific domains requiring trustworthy predictions. However, the research is at an early stage, and validation on complex real-world data remains necessary.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 희소 센서 데이터로부터 고차원 시공간 장(場)을 재구성하는 문제를 다룹니다. 기존의 SHRED(얕은 순환 디코더) 구조는 매우 적은 수의 센서로도 우수한 공간 정보 복원이 가능했지만, 복잡한 시스템이나 데이터가 부족한 환경에서 예측 불확실성을 정량화하지 못하는 한계가 있었습니다. 연구팀은 이를 보완한 UQ-SHRED를 제안하여 확률분포 기반의 불확실성 추정 기능을 추가했습니다. 이 방법은 기계학습 모델이 자신의 예측이 얼마나 신뢰할 수 있는지 수치로 제시할 수 있게 합니다. 이러한 접근은 기후 모델링, 난류 흐름 분석, 기타 과학 응용 분야에서 안정적인 의사결정을 가능하게 합니다. 다만, 본 연구는 아직 초기 단계로 실제 복잡한 현장 데이터에서의 성능 검증이 필요한 것으로 보입니다.

##

##
keyPoints: **기존 한계 극복** / **Overcoming Previous Limitations**: SHRED는 희소 센서로 효율적 복원이 가능하지만 예측 확실성을 제공하지 못했고, UQ-SHRED는 불확실성 정량화 기능 추가 / SHRED enables efficient reconstruction from sparse sensors but lacks uncertainty quantification; UQ-SHRED addresses this limitation

articles.title: Parallelized Hierarchical Connectome: A Spatiotemporal Recurrent Framework for Spiking State-Space Models
articles.titleKo: 병렬화된 계층적 연결체: 스파이킹 상태-공간 모델을 위한 시공간 순환 프레임워크
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.01295v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This work presents the Parallelized Hierarchical Connectome (PHC), a general framework that upgrades temporal-only State-Space Models (SSMs) into spatiotemporal recurrent networks. Conventional SSMs achieve high-speed sequence processing through parallel scans, yet are limited to temporal recurrence without lateral or feedback interactions within a single timestep. PHC maps the diagonal SSM core to a shared Neuron Layer and inter-neuronal communication to a shared Synapse Layer, where neurons are partitioned into hierarchical regions governed by
articles.aiSummary:
This research introduces the Parallelized Hierarchical Connectome (PHC), a novel neural network framework that enhances temporal-only State-Space Models (SSMs) to process both spatial and temporal information simultaneously. Conventional SSMs process sequential data at high speed but lack lateral and feedback interactions between neurons within a single timestep. The PHC framework applies neurobiology concepts by mapping SSM's diagonal core to a shared Neuron Layer and inter-neuronal communication to a shared Synapse Layer, organizing neurons into hierarchical regions. This approach creates spiking neural network models that more closely resemble biological brain structure while maintaining computational efficiency. The framework is expected to demonstrate improved performance compared to existing models across various spatiotemporal tasks. However, as this is preliminary research, extensive validation across diverse real-world datasets remains necessary.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

PHC는 신경 생물학의 개념을 적용하여 SSM의 대각선 코어를 신경층으로, 신경 간 통신을 시냅스층으로 매핑하며, 신경들을 계층적 영역으로 분할하는 구조를 제안합니다. 이 접근 방식은 생물학적 뇌의 구조와 더 유사하면서도 계산 효율성을 유지하는 스파이킹 신경망 모델을 가능하게 합니다. 다양한 시공간 작업에서 기존 모델 대비 개선된 성능을 보여주는 것으로 예상됩니다. 다만, 현재 결과는 논문의 초안 단계이며, 다양한 실제 데이터셋에 대한 광범위한 검증이 필요합니다.

##

articles.title: Interpretable Electrophysiological Features of Resting-State EEG Capture Cortical Network Dynamics in Parkinsons Disease
articles.titleKo: 파킨슨병의 대뇌피질 네트워크 역학을 포착하는 해석 가능한 휴지상태 뇌파의 전기생리학적 특징
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.01475v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Parkinsons disease (PD) alters cortical neural dynamics, yet reliable non-invasive electrophysiological biomarkers remain elusive. This study examined whether interpretable EEG features capturing complementary aspects of neural dynamics can discriminate Parkinsonian neural states. A comprehensive set of interpretable features was extracted and grouped into Standard descriptors (spectral power, phase synchronization, time-domain statistics) and Dynamical descriptors (aperiodic activity, cross-frequency coupling, scale-free dynamics, neuronal avala
articles.aiSummary:
Parkinson's disease (PD) alters cortical neural dynamics, but reliable non-invasive biomarkers to detect these changes remain lacking. This study investigated whether interpretable EEG features can effectively discriminate Parkinsonian neural states by extracting and categorizing features into two groups: Standard descriptors (spectral power, phase synchronization, time-domain statistics) and Dynamical descriptors (aperiodic activity, cross-frequency coupling, scale-free dynamics). The research presents a comprehensive approach to objectively measure abnormal neural dynamics in PD patients through interpretable features. The significance of this work lies in enabling clinicians to make more accurate diagnoses through easily interpretable neurophysiological markers. However, this appears to be an early-stage study that requires further clinical validation with larger patient populations and longitudinal follow-up data.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
파킨슨병은 뇌의 신경 활동 패턴을 변화시키지만, 이를 정확히 측정할 수 있는 비침습적 생체지표가 부족한 상황이다. 본 연구팀은 뇌파(EEG)에서 추출한 다양한 특징들이 파킨슨병 환자의 뇌 신경 상태를 구별할 수 있는지 조사했다. 연구진은 표준 기술자(주파수 분석, 뇌 영역 간 동기화, 시간 통계)와 역학적 기술자(비주기적 활동, 교차주파수 연결, 척도 자유 역학)를 포함한 포괄적인 뇌파 특징들을 추출하고 분류했다. 이는 파킨슨병 환자의 비정상적인 신경 역학을 객관적으로 진단하고 모니터링할 수 있는 새로운 방법을 제시한다. 연구의 의의는 신경과 의사가 쉽게 해석할 수 있는 특징들을 통해 더 정확한 진단을 가능하게 한다는 점이다. 다만 이 연구는 초기 단계이며, 더 많은 환자를 대상으로 한 임상 검증이 필요하다.

##

##
keyPoints: **신경 역학 측정**: 파킨슨병으로 인한 뇌 신경 활동의 변화를 표준 및 역학적 기술자를 통해 객관적으로 포착 / Capturing cortical neural dynamics alterations in PD through both standard and dynamical EEG descriptors
**해석 가능성**: 신경과 의사가 이해하고 임상에 직접 적용할 수 있는 해석 가능한 특징들 기반의 진단 / Clinically interpretable features that enable direct clinical application without requiring complex black-box algorithms
**다층적 분석**: 주파수 분석, 뇌 영역 간 동기화, 척도 자유 역학 등 신경 활동의 여러 측면을 종합적으로 분석 / Comprehensive analysis capturing multiple aspects of neural activity including spectral, synchronization, and scale-free properties

**임상 응용 가능성**: 파킨슨병 조기 진단, 질병 진행 모니터링, 치료 효과 평가 등에 활용 가능한 비침습적 생체지표 제시 / Potential for early diagnosis, disease progression monitoring, and treatment response assessment through non-invasive neurophysiological biomarkers

articles.title: Thermodynamic connectivity reveals functional specialization and multiplex organization of extrasynaptic signaling
articles.titleKo: 열역학적 연결성을 통해 드러나는 시냅스외 신호전달의 기능적 특화와 다중계층 구조
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.02057v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Neural communication operates on both fast synaptic transmission and slower, diffusive extrasynaptic signaling, yet how these two modes jointly organize brain function remains unclear. Here, using the complete synaptic and neuropeptidergic connectomes of \emph{Caenorhabditis elegans}, we develop a unified multiplex framework linking anatomical wiring to functional communication. We infer structure-derived functional connectivity from the synaptic connectome using equilibrium principles from statistical physics, yielding a probabilistic map of inf
articles.aiSummary:
This study investigates how two neural signaling modes—fast synaptic transmission and slower extrasynaptic diffusive signaling—jointly organize brain function. Researchers developed a unified multiplex framework using the complete synaptic and neuropeptidergic connectomes of *Caenorhabditis elegans*. By applying equilibrium principles from statistical physics, they inferred functional connectivity from anatomical wiring structure and analyzed how both signaling systems contribute to functional specialization and multiplex organization in neural circuits. This research presents a novel mathematical methodology linking anatomical connectivity to functional roles in neural networks. The study uses *C. elegans* as a model organism with a fully mapped connectome, which may limit direct application to more complex mammalian brains due to biological differences.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 뇌에서 일어나는 두 가지 신경 신호 전달 방식 - 빠른 시냅스 전달과 느린 확산성 시냅스 외 신호 전달 - 이 어떻게 함께 뇌 기능을 조직하는지 규명하기 위해 수행되었습니다. 연구팀은 선충류(예쁜꼬마선충류)의 완전한 시냅스 구조와 신경펩타이드 연결 지도를 이용하여 해석학적 프레임워크를 개발했습니다. 통계물리학의 평형 원리를 적용하여 시냅스 연결 구조로부터 기능적 연결성을 추론하고, 두 신호 전달 체계가 뇌의 기능 전문화(functional specialization)와 다층 조직(multiplex organization)을 어떻게 형성하는지 분석했습니다. 이 연구는 신경 회로의 해부학적 구조와 실제 기능적 역할 간의 관계를 수학적으로 연결하는 새로운 방법론을 제시합니다. 완전한 연결체(connectome) 데이터를 가진 선충류를 모델로 사용했기 때문에 포유류 뇌에 직접 적용하기에는 생물학적 복잡성의 차이가 있을 수 있습니다.

##

articles.title: Different types of childhood maltreatment appear to uniquely shape human brain development
articles.titleKo: 다양한 유형의 아동학대가 인간의 뇌 발달을 고유하게 형성하는 것으로 보인다
articles.summary: A large new study published in Biological Psychiatry reveals that childhood abuse and neglect are associated with distinct differences in adult brain structure. These physical variations are most pronounced in young women and differ based on trauma type.
articles.aiSummary:
This large-scale study published in Biological Psychiatry investigates how different types of childhood maltreatment affect adult brain structure. Researchers conducted MRI scans and analyzed the brains of adults who experienced various forms of trauma including physical abuse, emotional abuse, and neglect during childhood. The findings reveal that different types of maltreatment are associated with distinct alterations in different brain regions, with these structural changes being particularly pronounced in young women. For instance, brain changes in individuals who experienced physical abuse differed significantly from those who experienced neglect. This research demonstrates that childhood trauma can produce long-term physical changes in brain structure, highlighting the need for trauma-type-specific therapeutic interventions for abuse survivors. However, the study has limitations including potential confounding variables related to participant demographics and other environmental factors that may not have been fully controlled.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 아동기 학대와 방임이 성인의 뇌 구조에 어떤 영향을 미치는지 조사한 대규모 연구입니다. 연구팀은 아동기에 신체적 학대, 정서적 학대, 방임 등 다양한 형태의 트라우마를 경험한 성인들의 뇌를 MRI로 촬영하여 분석했습니다. 연구 결과, 학대 유형에 따라 뇌의 서로 다른 영역이 변화하는 것으로 나타났으며, 이러한 뇌 구조 변화는 특히 젊은 여성들에게서 더욱 뚜렷했습니다. 예를 들어, 신체적 학대를 경험한 사람과 방임을 경험한 사람의 뇌 변화 패턴은 상당히 달랐습니다. 이 연구는 아동 트라우마가 장기적으로 뇌의 물리적 구조를 변화시킨다는 것을 입증하여, 아동 학대 피해자들을 위한 맞춤형 심리 치료 개발의 필요성을 제시합니다. 다만, 연구 참가자의 구성이나 다른 환경 요인들이 완전히 통제되지 않았을 수 있다는 한계가 있습니다.

articles.title: Brain scans shed light on how short videos impair memory and alter neural pathways
articles.titleKo: 뇌 스캔이 짧은 영상이 어떻게 기억력을 손상시키고 신경 경로를 변화시키는지 밝혀내다
articles.summary: A recent brain imaging experiment reveals that watching fragmented short videos leads to measurably worse memory recall compared to viewing continuous content. The fast-paced format reduces brain activity in regions dedicated to focusing attention and processing deep meaning.
articles.aiSummary:
Recent brain imaging research reveals that watching fragmented short videos impairs memory recall by reducing neural activity in brain regions responsible for attention and deep information processing. Researchers conducted an fMRI experiment where participants watched either short-format fragmented videos or continuous long-form content while their brain activity was measured and compared. Results showed significantly lower memory recall rates in the short-video group compared to the long-form group, with substantially decreased activation in the prefrontal cortex and temporal lobe regions involved in attention and semantic processing. This study has implications for understanding how rapid, fragmentary video formats interfere with meaningful information processing at the neurological level. However, limitations include the lack of consideration for individual differences (age, ADHD tendencies) and the need for further research on long-term effects of short-video consumption.

##

최근 뇌 영상 연구는 짧은 영상 콘텐츠 시청이 뇌의 주의집중 및 의미 처리 영역의 활동을 감소시켜 기억력을 저하시킨다는 것을 밝혔습니다. 연구팀은 참가자들에게 단편화된 짧은 영상과 연속적인 긴 형식의 콘텐츠를 보여주고 fMRI 뇌 영상 기술로 뇌 활동을 비교 측정했습니다. 결과적으로 짧은 영상 시청군이 긴 형식 콘텐츠 시청군에 비해 현저히 낮은 기억 회상률을 보였으며, 특히 전전두피질과 측두엽 등 주의력과 의미 처리를 담당하는 뇌 영역의 활성화가 크게 감소했습니다. 이 연구는 빠르게 변화하는 짧은 영상 포맷이 깊이 있는 정보 처리를 방해하는 메커니즘을 신경과학적으로 규명했다는 점에서 의의가 있습니다. 다만 연구에서 다루지 않은 개인차(예: 나이, 주의력 결핍 성향) 및 장기적 효과에 대한 추가 연구가 필요합니다.

articles.title: Facts and the Fight for Moral High Ground
articles.summary: In southwest Philadelphia in August 2019, a young Black man sat down on a folding chair in a high school auditorium and clipped a lapel mic to a bright yellow T-shirt that read “Philly Thrive.” “So, how you doin’? My name is Ricky,” he said into a camera. “I’m a resident in the Gray’s Ferry community, and I’m hoping and praying the refinery gets shutdown.” The refinery in question was Philadelphia Energy Solutions (PES), up to that point the largest oil refinery on the Eastern Seaboard. It had operated just a stone’s throw from the Gray’s Ferry neighborhood until two months earlier. In the ear
articles.aiSummary:
This article examines a 2019 environmental justice movement in Philadelphia's Gray's Ferry neighborhood, where residents advocated for the shutdown of Philadelphia Energy Solutions (PES), the largest oil refinery on the Eastern Seaboard. The research analyzes how facts and narratives are strategically used to establish "moral high ground" in environmental activism through community testimonies and media documentation. Key findings reveal that residents constructed persuasive narratives not solely through scientific facts but by integrating personal experiences and collective community voices. The study demonstrates how emotional appeals and factual arguments interact and reinforce each other in public health advocacy. The significance lies in showing that community voice and trust-building are often more effective than information provision alone in environmental justice campaigns. A limitation is that this is a case study from a specific geographic location, which may restrict generalization to broader environmental movements.

articles.aiSummary:
This article covers a podcast episode about the daily experiences and challenges of power line workers (linemen). Bestselling author Dan Heath interviews Elden Rivas, an experienced journeyman lineman in Houston, Texas, through a conversational format. The methodology is qualitative interview-based, with no specific participant numbers or experimental design mentioned. Key topics include post-tornado neighborhood power restoration, helicopter-assisted line work in Faraday suits, and survival of a collapsed utility pole incident. The podcast aims to educate the public about hazardous occupations and increase understanding of essential infrastructure workers. However, as a personal interview rather than scientific research, the generalizability of findings is limited.

articles.title: Study finds dangerous lead levels in children’s clothing
articles.titleKo: 연구에 따르면 어린이 의류에서 위험한 수준의 납이 검출되다
articles.summary: Fast fashion might come with a hidden danger: lead. Researchers testing children’s shirts from multiple retailers found every sample exceeded U.S. safety limits, raising concerns about toxic exposure—especially since young kids often chew on clothing. Brightly colored fabrics like red and yellow showed particularly high levels, likely due to chemicals used to fix dyes. Simulations suggest that even brief mouthing could expose children to unsafe amounts of lead, a substance known to harm brain development and behavior.
articles.aiSummary:
This research investigated lead contamination in fast fashion children's clothing and its potential health risks. Researchers collected children's shirt samples from multiple retailers and measured their lead content, finding that all samples exceeded U.S. safety standards. Notably, brightly colored fabrics like red and yellow showed particularly high lead levels, likely due to chemical dyes used to fix colors to the fabric. Computer simulations demonstrated that even brief mouthing of clothing could expose children to unsafe lead levels that surpass safety thresholds. Since lead is known to harm brain development and behavioral development, the findings raise significant health concerns. However, the study was limited to samples from specific retailers, and more comprehensive research across broader product ranges is necessary to fully understand the scope of this problem.

articles.title: Millions start work too early. This drug helps them stay awake
articles.titleKo: 수백만 명이 너무 일찍 일을 시작한다. 이 약이 깨어있는 데 도움을 준다
articles.summary: Millions of people start work before sunrise—but their brains aren’t ready for it. A new clinical trial has found that the wake-promoting drug solriamfetol can significantly boost alertness in early-morning shift workers struggling with shift work disorder. Participants who took the drug were able to stay awake and function better throughout full shifts, with improvements in productivity, safety, and daily performance.
articles.aiSummary:
Millions of workers beginning their shifts before sunrise face the challenge of starting work when their brains are not yet alert. A recent clinical trial found that solriamfetol, a wake-promoting drug, significantly enhances alertness in early-morning shift workers suffering from shift work disorder. Participants who received the drug were able to maintain wakefulness throughout their entire shifts and demonstrated improved functioning, with notable gains in productivity, safety, and overall daily performance. This research is significant as it offers a pharmacological solution to the chronic sleep deprivation problem experienced by shift workers. However, limitations include the need for further investigation into long-term drug side effects, variability in effectiveness across different populations, and the dependency on medication rather than addressing the underlying work schedule issues.

articles.title: Cannabis intoxication broadly impairs multiple memory types, new study shows
articles.titleKo: 대마초 중독이 여러 유형의 기억력을 광범위하게 손상시킨다는 새로운 연구 결과
articles.summary: A new study in the Journal of Psychopharmacology mapped how cannabis impacts human cognition. The findings provide evidence that intoxication broadly disrupts multiple memory domains, increasing false memories and making it harder to recall practical, everyday information.
articles.aiSummary:
A new study published in the Journal of Psychopharmacology investigated how cannabis consumption affects human cognitive abilities, particularly memory. The research provides evidence that cannabis intoxication broadly impairs multiple types of memory across different domains. Specifically, cannabis users demonstrated increased susceptibility to forming false memories—remembering events that never actually occurred—and showed diminished ability to recall practical, everyday information necessary for daily functioning. This study clarifies that cannabis affects not just attention but has widespread impacts on the entire memory system, providing important evidence for drug policy and public health education. However, the lack of specific sample sizes and concrete numerical data limits deeper analysis of the findings.

##

articles.title: Large-scale study links autoimmune diseases to higher rates of depression and anxiety
articles.summary: A sweeping analysis of over a million health records reveals that people living with autoimmune diseases experience depression and anxiety at nearly twice the rate of the general public, suggesting a profound link between physical inflammation and mental health.
articles.aiSummary:
A large-scale study analyzing over one million health records has revealed a significant link between autoimmune diseases and mental health disorders. Researchers found that people with autoimmune diseases experience depression and anxiety at nearly twice the rate of the general population. This finding suggests that physical inflammation from autoimmune conditions may profoundly influence mental health outcomes. The research underscores the importance of integrated mental health care for autoimmune disease patients and highlights the need for a holistic treatment approach addressing both physical and psychological symptoms. However, the study demonstrates correlation rather than establishing clear causation, and may not fully account for individual variations and socio-cultural factors that could influence the results.

##

articles.title: A gene mutation may trap the brain in the wrong reality in schizophrenia patients
articles.titleKo: 유전자 돌연변이가 정신분열증 환자의 뇌를 잘못된 현실에 갇히게 할 수 있다
articles.summary: A newly identified gene mutation may help explain why schizophrenia patients struggle to update their understanding of reality. The mutation disrupts a brain circuit involved in flexible decision-making, causing mice to stick with outdated choices even when conditions change. Researchers pinpointed the issue to a key thalamus–prefrontal cortex pathway. By reactivating this circuit, they were able to restore normal behavior—raising hope for future therapies.
articles.aiSummary:
A newly identified gene mutation has been discovered that may explain why schizophrenia patients struggle to accurately perceive reality. Researchers found that this mutation disrupts a brain circuit responsible for flexible decision-making, and experiments with mice revealed that they persisted with outdated choices even when environmental conditions changed. Specifically, the neural pathway connecting the thalamus and prefrontal cortex was damaged, but reactivating this circuit restored normal behavior in the mice. This discovery represents an important step in understanding the neurobiological causes of schizophrenia and offers significant hope for future drug development and therapeutic improvements. However, additional research is needed to confirm whether results from animal experiments can be equally applied to humans. Furthermore, since schizophrenia results from multiple genetic and environmental factors working together, a single gene mutation alone cannot provide a complete explanation.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
조현병 환자들이 현실을 올바르게 인식하지 못하는 이유를 설명할 수 있는 새로운 유전자 돌연변이가 발견되었다. 연구팀은 이 돌연변이가 뇌의 유연한 의사결정을 담당하는 회로를 방해한다는 것을 밝혀냈으며, 실험쥐를 통해 환경이 변해도 과거의 선택에 집착하는 행동을 확인했다. 구체적으로 시상과 전전두피질을 연결하는 신경회로 경로가 손상되어 있었으며, 이 회로를 다시 활성화시키자 정상적인 행동이 회복되었다. 이 발견은 조현병의 신경생물학적 원인을 이해하는 데 중요한 단계이며, 향후 신약 개발과 치료법 개선에 큰 희망을 제공한다. 다만 동물 실험 결과가 인간에게 동일하게 적용될 수 있는지 확인하는 추가 연구가 필요하다. 또한 조현병은 다양한 유전적·환경적 요인이 복합적으로 작용하므로 단일 유전자만으로는 완전한 설명이 어렵다.

articles.title: Eating more meat may lower Alzheimer’s risk for some people
articles.titleKo: 더 많은 육류 섭취가 일부 사람들의 알츠하이머병 위험을 낮출 수 있다
articles.summary: A surprising new study suggests that genetics may change how diet affects brain health—especially when it comes to Alzheimer’s risk. Researchers found that older adults carrying high-risk APOE gene variants didn’t show the expected cognitive decline if they ate relatively high amounts of meat. In fact, those with these genes who consumed the most meat had slower cognitive decline and lower dementia risk, challenging conventional dietary advice.
articles.aiSummary:
A recent study investigates how genetics regulate the impact of diet on brain health, particularly regarding Alzheimer's risk. Researchers examined older adults carrying high-risk APOE gene variants and analyzed the relationship between meat consumption and cognitive decline. Surprisingly, among those with these high-risk genes, individuals who consumed the most meat showed slower cognitive decline and lower dementia risk, contrary to conventional dietary recommendations. This finding suggests that the same diet can have different effects depending on an individual's genetic characteristics. The research challenges the one-size-fits-all approach to nutrition and dementia prevention. However, further validation is needed regarding sample size, follow-up duration, and other dietary factors.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
최근 연구는 유전자가 식단이 뇌 건강에 미치는 영향을 어떻게 조절하는지를 조사했습니다. 연구팀은 알츠하이머병 고위험 유전자(APOE)를 보유한 노인들을 대상으로 육류 섭취량과 인지 기능 저하의 관계를 분석했습니다. 놀랍게도 이 고위험 유전자를 가진 사람들 중 육류를 많이 섭취한 그룹이 더 느린 인지 기능 저하를 보였고 치매 위험도 더 낮았습니다. 이는 일반적인 식단 권장사항과 상충되는 결과입니다. 이 연구는 개인의 유전적 특성에 따라 같은 식단이 서로 다른 영향을 미칠 수 있음을 시사합니다. 다만 표본 크기, 추적 기간, 기타 식생활 요인 등에 대한 추가 검증이 필요합니다.

articles.title: How Emotion Shapes the Behavior of LLMs and Agents: A Mechanistic Study
articles.titleKo: 감정이 대규모 언어 모델과 에이전트의 행동을 형성하는 방식: 기계론적 연구
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.00005v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Emotion plays an important role in human cognition and performance. Motivated by this, we investigate whether analogous emotional signals can shape the behavior of large language models (LLMs) and agents. Existing emotion-aware studies mainly treat emotion as a surface-level style factor or a perception target, overlooking its mechanistic role in task processing. articles.aiSummary:
This study investigates whether emotional signals can shape the behavior of large language models (LLMs) and AI agents, inspired by the significant role emotions play in human cognition and performance. While previous emotion-aware studies treated emotion only as a surface-level style factor or perception target, this research aims to understand its mechanistic role in task processing. The researchers propose E-STEER, an interpretable emotion steering framework that enables direct representation-level intervention in LLMs and agents rather than relying on prompt-based approaches. This approach demonstrates that emotions can substantively alter the internal working mechanisms of AI models. The significance of this work lies in deepening our understanding of AI model behavior and suggesting possibilities for performance enhancement through emotional control. However, the research is in its early stages, and further validation across diverse models and complex tasks is necessary.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

articles.title: One Panel Does Not Fit All: Case-Adaptive Multi-Agent Deliberation for Clinical Prediction
articles.titleKo: 하나의 패널로는 모두를 충족할 수 없다: 임상 예측을 위한 사례 적응형 다중 에이전트 협의
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.00085v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models applied to clinical prediction exhibit case-level heterogeneity: simple cases yield consistent outputs, while complex cases produce divergent predictions under minor prompt changes. Existing single-agent strategies sample from one role-conditioned distribution, and multi-agent frameworks use fixed roles with flat majority voting, discarding the diagnostic signal in disagreement. We propose CAMP (Case-Adaptive Multi-agent Panel), where an attending-physician agent dynamically assembles a specialist panel tailored to each case
articles.aiSummary:
This study addresses the inconsistency problem in clinical predictions made by large language models (LLMs), where simple cases produce reliable outputs but complex cases generate divergent predictions with minor prompt variations. Traditional single-agent LLM strategies and fixed-role multi-agent frameworks discard diagnostic signals present in disagreements among experts. The researchers propose CAMP (Case-Adaptive Multi-agent Panel), where an attending-physician agent dynamically assembles a specialist panel customized for each individual case. This approach distinguishes between simple and complex cases, automatically selecting the optimal combination of expert specialties to improve diagnostic accuracy. The research has significant implications for enhancing the reliability of AI-based clinical diagnosis systems, though validation in real clinical settings remains necessary.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

articles.title: Open, Reliable, and Collective: A Community-Driven Framework for Tool-Using AI Agents
articles.titleKo: 공개적이고 신뢰할 수 있는 협력적 접근: 도구 활용 AI 에이전트를 위한 커뮤니티 주도 프레임워크
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.00137v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Tool-integrated LLMs can retrieve, compute, and take real-world actions via external tools, but reliability remains a key bottleneck. We argue that failures stem from both tool-use accuracy (how well an agent invokes a tool) and intrinsic tool accuracy (the tool's own correctness), while most prior work emphasizes the former. We introduce OpenTools, a community-driven toolbox that standardizes tool schemas, provides lightweight plug-and-play wrappers, and evaluates tools with automated test suites and continuous monitoring. We also release a publ
articles.aiSummary:
This research addresses the reliability challenges when large language models (LLMs) utilize external tools for real-world actions. While previous studies focused primarily on how well AI agents invoke tools, this research argues that both tool-use accuracy (agent performance) and intrinsic tool accuracy (tool correctness itself) are equally critical. The team developed OpenTools, a community-driven toolbox framework that provides standardized tool schemas, lightweight plug-and-play wrappers, automated test suites, and continuous monitoring capabilities. The framework aims to create an open, reliable, and collectively-maintained ecosystem for tool-integrated AI systems. The significance lies in presenting a systematic, standardized approach to addressing tool reliability issues in AI agents. However, specific quantitative performance metrics are not provided in the abstract, which may limit empirical validation of the framework's effectiveness.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
